description:
GOAL: Heap-allocated vectors with raw contiguous payloads. SIMD-friendly. Unblocks fz-ul4.14 (SIMD codegen).

## Decisions captured

- Per-element-kind: HeapKind::VecI64 / VecF64 / VecU8 / VecBit. Kind is in the header, NOT a discriminant byte in the payload (keeps payload pure raw data, faster SIMD).
- Layout: HeapHeader (16) + len:u32 (4) + padding (4) + raw_payload (16-byte aligned for SIMD).
- NON-MOVING collector (.11.2 design choice) ensures stable addresses — SIMD codegen in .14 can rely on this.
- Builtins (already in interp): vec_get / vec_map / vec_reduce / etc. Wire to IR prims.
- ~v[1, 2, 3] / ~b[0xff] / ~bits[1, 0, 1, 1] sigils -> Prim::AllocVec(kind, [elements]).

## Deliverables

- IR prims for vec alloc / get / per-kind ops.
- Codegen for each kind.
- Tracer branches for VecI64/F64/U8/Bit in .11.2 (no FzValue inside; nothing to trace, just account for size).

## Tests

- `~v[1, 2, 3]` allocates a Vec(I64).
- `~v[1.0, 2.0]` allocates a Vec(F64).
- `~b[0xff, 0xab]` allocates a Vec(U8).
- `~bits[1, 0, 1, 1]` allocates a Vec(Bit).
- vec_map and vec_reduce work via JIT (currently interp-only via builtins).
- Pinned-stable address: allocate + GC + read still works (proves non-moving GC).
- Existing vec fixtures (from main.rs SAMPLE constant) work via JIT.

## Unblocks

- fz-ul4.14 SIMD codegen.

comments.text:
Authorized 2026-05-10: option (a) split. Land VecI64/VecU8/VecBit + sigil alloc + vec_get + GC-stable read here.

Deferred to follow-ups (both filed):
- fz-ul4.11.22 — vec_map/vec_reduce via JIT (needs runtime->JIT trampoline re-entry seam; nontrivial architectural addition)
- fz-ul4.11.23 — VecF64 storage and ~v[1.0, 2.0] (blocked by .11.20 boxed floats)

Remaining .11.14 scope after split: I64/U8/Bit literal alloc, render, vec_get, pinned-stable address GC test.

Implementation notes:

- VecKindIr bifurcated from ast::VecKind alias into its own enum (I64/F64/U8/Bit). lower_vec_lit picks I64 for Numeric (or errors with .11.23 reference if any element is Float), U8 for Bytes, Bit for Bits.
- Heap layout per ticket: HeapHeader(16) + len:u32(4) + pad:u32(4) + raw_payload (16-rounded total). Single Heap::alloc_vec_raw helper; thin per-kind wrappers (alloc_vec_i64/u8/bit) write payload bytes after header. Heap::vec_len is a static reader.
- Bit packing matches bitstr::BitWriter (MSB-first within each byte) so the storage is interchangeable with bitstrings.
- BuiltinKind enum added in fz_ir as the typed view of BuiltinId; BuiltinTable derived from BuiltinKind::ALL so name->id mapping is single-sourced. Codegen dispatches Prim::Builtin by BuiltinKind, retiring the print-only  check.
- vec_get wired as BuiltinKind::VecGet -> fz_vec_get runtime fn (decodes per-kind, returns NIL on out-of-bounds).
- Tests: 4 heap-layer (I64/U8/Bit alloc, GC address stability) + 7 ir_codegen (print I64/U8/Bit, VecF64 lowering gates with .11.23 pointer, vec_get hit, vec_get OOB-as-nil, GC-stable address via JIT halt root).

Deferred (filed):
- fz-ul4.11.22 — vec_map / vec_reduce via JIT (runtime->JIT trampoline re-entry).
- fz-ul4.11.23 — VecF64 storage and ~v[1.0, 2.0] (blocked by .11.20).

title: Vec types as heap types (unblocks .14 SIMD)
